decisions.briefing: Markets dipped modestly today with NASDAQ off 0.4% and S&P 500 down 0.2% in a neutral, low-conviction session. Cash is at $477.14 — just below the $500 threshold required to open new positions, so no buys are triggered. Existing positions continue to hold with the portfolio up a strong +31.6% overall; will look to deploy into top-ranked candidates like FDS or PSN if cash recovers above the threshold.

market_education.summary_en: U.S. equities closed slightly lower on June 26, 2026, with the NASDAQ falling 0.4% and the S&P 500 down 0.2%, reflecting a broad lack of directional conviction heading into the end of the month. Sector rotation and light volume ahead of quarter-end rebalancing likely pressured growth names. No major macro catalysts drove the session, leaving markets in a wait-and-see posture.
market_education.summary_zh: 2026年6月26日，美國股市小幅收跌，那斯達克指數下滑0.4%，標普500指數微跌0.2%，整體走勢缺乏明確方向。市場人士指出，季末資產再平衡及板塊輪動是當日主要壓力來源，成長類股受到一定拖累。由於缺乏重大宏觀催化劑，市場整體維持觀望態度。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Cash drag refers to the performance penalty a portfolio suffers when it holds too much cash instead of investing. Today's situation illustrates it in reverse — the portfolio is just $23 short of the $500 cash threshold needed to open a new position, meaning good buying opportunities (FDS, PSN at score 95) are effectively locked out. Keeping a small but purposeful cash buffer is smart for risk management, but idle cash that misses rallies quietly erodes relative returns over time.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「現金拖累」是指投資組合因持有過多現金而未能充分投資，導致相對收益受損的現象。今日情況恰好說明了這一點——組合現金僅差23美元便達到開倉所需的500美元門檻，使得評分高達95的優質標的（如FDS、PSN）錯失入場時機。保留適度的現金緩衝有助於風險管理，但長期閒置的現金在市場上漲時會悄悄蠶食組合的相對報酬。
